_doc: Verbatim, scrubbed responses captured from the original uipath-troubleshoot session that diagnosed a Faulted GSuite Write Range job in folder Shared. Job/folder keys, the malformed A1 range (Sheet1!A0), and all exception/stack text are preserved exactly; hostname, email, and Windows account were scrubbed (UIP-PW06WJSK -> MOCK-HOST, dan.morosanu@uipath.com -> original_email@test.com, UIPATH\DAN.MOROSANU -> UIPATH\REPLACEMENT_USER). First-match-wins: specific patterns precede generic ones. `is connections list` is a DEFENSIVE fixture proving both Shared GSuite connections are valid/Enabled (the fault is NOT a connection problem). `or jobs traces` returns the real non-JSON Failure envelope the live CLI produced (traces unavailable).

unmocked_default._doc: Any command with no matching rule returns an empty JSON array with exit 0, mirroring an empty list result. Keeps the agent from hard-failing on an unrecorded probe.

expected_calls.description: Agent lists folders to resolve folder Shared to its key (1965a46b-...).
Agent finds the most-recent Faulted job in Shared (d5e0e6b0 is the latest by StartTime).
Agent reads the faulted job's Info, exposing the GSuiteException / Google 400 BadRequest stack and the malformed range Sheet1!A0.
OPTIONAL — Agent may list connections to rule out a connection/auth problem; the fixture shows both GSuite connections Enabled. Tracked-but-not-required.
OPTIONAL — traces are unavailable for this job (non-JSON Failure envelope); tracked-but-not-required.
